summary: Large decompilation pass for Dr. Mario's pill item. The PR added most of src/melee/it/items/itdrmariopill.c behavior, introduced Dr. Mario pill item variable and attribute structs in itCharItems.h, and wired the pill item vars into the Item union in it/types.h. The implementation covers pill spawn/throw velocity setup, animation/state transitions, gravity/fall setup, owner/fighter linkage, scale/position syncing with the fighter, collision/despawn behavior, and several repeated hit/collision callbacks. Evidence is mostly from the diff; there were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added Dr. Mario pill-specific item vars: itDrMarioPill_ItemVars with fields at item data offsets ip+DD4 through ip+DE0, including a stored HSD_GObj pointer at x4.
Added itDrMarioPillAttributes with five f32 fields x0, x4, x8, xC, and x10, used through xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Added itDrMarioPill_ItemVars drmariopill to the Item xDD4 item variable union.
Expanded itdrmariopill.c from a small stub into a substantial decompilation containing local helpers and item callbacks for Dr. Mario pill behavior.
Added a GET_ATTRS(ip) macro to cast article special attributes to itDrMarioPillAttributes.
Implemented a local my_sqrtf using __frsqrte and four Newton-style refinement iterations, with a note that this differs from the usual three iterations.
Implemented state/animation helpers such as it_802C1180, it_802C0B5C, it_802C0DF8, and it_802C11E0 using it_80275158, Item_80268E5C, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.

Implemented physics/collision-related callbacks that apply fall speed, update item animation/accessory behavior, test item collision via it_8027781C, compute velocity magnitude, compare against attribute x10, and play effect/sound-like event 0x15FAE via Item_8026AE84.
Implemented owner/fighter-linked pill behavior using ftDr_Init_80149844, ftDr_Init_801498A0, ftDr_Init_801498EC, ftLib_80086644, ftLib_800869D4, and ftCo_800BF228.
Implemented JObj manipulation for pill visual sync, including translate, rotation Y, child visibility flags, and scale updates.
Changed it_802C1570 signature from taking Item_GObj* ref_gobj to taking HSD_GObj* hobj, then forwards to it_8026B894.
smart_moves: Kept Dr. Mario pill-specific per-item data in a named itDrMarioPill_ItemVars struct and added it to the central Item union, avoiding anonymous xDD4 field use in the implementation.
Centralized article special attribute casting with GET_ATTRS(ip), making repeated access to xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes less error-prone.
Documented matching-sensitive math behavior: the custom sqrt helper uses four __frsqrte refinement iterations instead of the usual three.
Preserved matching-oriented duplicated callbacks instead of over-refactoring them; several functions are explicitly noted as identical to it_802C1384, which can matter for codegen.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in functions where stack layout likely mattered for matching.
Modeled the pill's attachment to Dr. Mario through the item var gobj pointer and explicitly clears on_accessory and the stored fighter pointer during detach/cleanup paths.
decomp_lessons: For character-specific items, add named item-var structs in itCharItems.h and wire them into the Item union before replacing xDD4 raw field access.
Article special attributes can often be represented as small offset-only structs first; meaningful field names can wait until behavior is better understood.
Matching math may require reproducing nonstandard libm behavior, such as hand-written sqrt using __frsqrte and a precise number of refinement iterations.
Do not automatically merge identical-looking item callbacks; repeated functions may need to remain separate for symbol/table layout or matching.
Fighter-owned item articles often need careful cleanup when the fighter owner no longer matches or fighter state changes; clear both the item-var fighter pointer and accessory callback state.
Visual article behavior may require direct HSD_JObj operations for child flags, scale, rotation, and translation rather than only item system helpers.
naming_conventions: PR follows existing decomp placeholder naming for unknown functions, e.g. it_802C0B5C, it_802C0E48, it_802C1384.
Character item structs use the pattern itDrMarioPill_ItemVars and itDrMarioPillAttributes.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-based names such as x0, x4, x8, xC, and x10, with offset comments where known.
Item union member name uses lowercase character/item style: drmariopill.
Macro name GET_ATTRS is local and returns a typed special-attributes pointer for the current item.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a custom inline my_sqrtf around __frsqrte with four refinement iterations to reproduce expected floating-point codegen.
Inserted PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(40) in several functions, indicating stack padding was needed for matching.
Kept redundant-looking calls to it_8026B3A8 in functions such as it_802C0DF8 and related callbacks, likely because call count/order affects matching.
Preserved goto-style exit flow in it_802C0E48, which can be useful for matching original branch structure.
Used explicit f64 constants and casts for rotation computation, e.g. 1.5707963267948966 multiplied by an item-var field.
Repeated near-identical callback bodies rather than abstracting common logic, preserving original function boundaries.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review and one issue comment according to counts, but their bodies were not included in the supplied excerpt, so reviewer rationale cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename itDrMarioPillAttributes fields x0/x4/x8/xC/x10 to semantic names.
Check whether it_802C1384, it_802C1400, it_802C145C, it_802C14B8, and it_802C1514 later remain duplicated or get better callback names.
Search for other item PRs using custom __frsqrte sqrt helpers to compare iteration counts and matching tactics.
Find later changes to it_802C1570 or it_8026B894 to confirm whether HSD_GObj* was the final correct type.
Review full PR comments if available outside this slice to capture any reviewer feedback omitted here.
